s. f. 1. Drunkenness, intoxication. 2. Lust, wantonness, pride.
subs: (-ko), To be affected with lust, or pride, &c.
or mastī jhāṛnā (-kī), To take the lust, or the pride, out (of a person), to bring (one) to his senses, to castigate.

Oh re terī bebe ne goḍūṅ! tamākhū ke saë phīm ne bhī māt kare saë , Rus. I could—your sister! Such tobacco! it beats even opium hollow. 2. To checkmate. māt khānā, yā h. , v. n. To be defeated, worsted.

1. s. f. Checkmate. 2. adj. Astonished, confounded, outdone. مات کرنا māt karnā, To win, outdo, to beat (at chess).

def: s.f. Accent; a vowel, &c. (see mātrā).

(as last member of compounds), Little; mere; in the measure or amount of, so much as; only, merely, nothing but, solely, wholly; even; just (e.g. ćhin-mātr, 'as much as a moment, only a moment'; manushya-mātr, 'only a man, a mere man').

ety: Prk. म + (prob.) अदि (3rd pers. sing. of the pres. of the auxiliary verb 'to be'); S. मा+अति
def: adv. (used with the imperat. only), Do not, don't (e.g. mat jā, 'don't go').
more: adv. S. मा Do not; dont. 1. Mat jā! Dont go! 2. Nandī ke bīrā, kuārī se mat bolyo! Kuārī halad kī sī geṅṭh, alag desī phoṛyo! Ratvāī. O brother of my sister-in-law! dont to a virgin speak! A virgin's brittle turmeric, do you far from her keep!

n. f. S. मति 1. Understanding ( samajh , 2). Sab dunyā ho gaī taṅg, huī mat-bhaṅg, phiran lage naṅg, baṛhe āghorī. Yeh jahāṅ tahāṅ gaë, hon lagī chorī . Kāl kā gīt. A song of the famine. Distracted all, of sense bereft, or naked, or foul clad, Deserted homes, all scattered wide, despoiled by each footpad. 2. Faith; belief; creed. matā k. , v. n. To lay heads together; conspire. mat phirnā , v. n. To change one's opinion. mat denā , v. a. To counsel; give advice. mat mārī jānā, mat kumat h. , v. n. To lose one's senses. Panghaṭ ko hamārī agar aswārī gaī hai To vāṅ bhī lagī sāth yehī khuārī gaī hai, Sunte haiṅ ki kahtī huī panihārī gaī hai, 'Lo dekho! buṛhāpe meṅ ye mat mārī gaī haī .' On old age. Nazīr. If to the well where women water draw I ride There too detraction rideth ever by my side, I'm told that as these fair ones go away they say, 'Just look at that old fool—his wits are gone a…
s. f. 1. Manner, method, way, mode, system. 2. Wisdom, understanding, intellect, opinion. مت هين mati-hīn, Void of understanding. 3. (Sans. मा Do not) neg. particle, Not, do not.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀫𑀁𑀢 (maṃta)), an extension of Ashokan Prakrit 𑀫𑀸 (mā), from Sanskrit मा (mā́), from Proto-Indo-Iranian *máH, from Proto-Indo-European *méh₁.

def: s.f. Mother; — an affectionate or respectful term of address to a woman (syn. mā, māʼī); — the small-pox (so named from the goddess Devī; cf. sītalā)
more: n. f. 1. See ما (1). 1. Mātā pāle kar-ke lāḍ, Sāsū chāhe toṛūṅ hāḍ . The mother o'er her baby fondly croons, The mother-in-law would like to break its bones. 2. Sirhāne baiṭhī mātā jhure, khes khinḍāë gorī, Kyoṅ jhurve bande Sāhab ke, jin joṛī un toṛī . Bhajan. Kabīr. The mother weeps beside the bed, the widow tears her hair, God's children ye, why grieve? He hath disjoined who you did pair. 2. (Sant. guṭi ) Small-pox ( sītlā ). mātā pitā, māt pitā , n. m. 1. Parents. 2. Lord; master. mātā pūjnā , v. a. To worship the goddess of small-pox. mātā kā maṅḍh , n. m. A small dome dedicated to the goddess of small-pox. mātā sītlā ke din . An epidemic of small-pox. mātā nikalnā , v. n. To come out (small-pox).

def: s.f. Firmness, fixedness; constancy; solidity; obstinacy; — depth (of thought); force, vigour (of style); — a fortification, castle, stronghold.
more: n. f. متن 1. Solidity; depth (of thought). 2. Force, vigor (of style). Inke kalām meṅ baṛī matānat hai .
(from متن) s. f. 1. Firmness, solidity. 2. A fortification, a castle. 3. Obstinacy.

def: s.m. (f. -ī), An elephant; — the sacred fig-tree Ficus religiosa; — epithet of the twenty-fourth astronomical Yoga or division of the moon's path; — a Ćanḍāl, a man of the lowest caste, an outcast; a barbarian.
s.m. An elephant; — a cloud.
s.m. Name of a form of Durgā

subs: v.n. (dialec.) To be confounded, or perplexed

def: s.m. (Dakh.) = matārā, q.v. s.m. (Dakh.) Property; an article of property; wealth, substance; merchandise.
s.m. A large species of pea; a large pea; — a kind of (spotted) silk cloth.
more: s. m. 1. A kind of pea. 2. Name of a kind of silk-cloth.

def: s.f. A mother (syn. mahtārī).

(gen. sing. of mata, q.v.), Of the mind, &c. ( = H. mat-kā).

def: s.m. (Braj) = matā, and mat or mati, qq.v. s.f. Mother; — an affectionate or respectful term of address to a woman (syn. mā, māʼī); — the small-pox (so named from the goddess Devī; cf. sītalā)
more: s. m. (in Braj) for متا matā, q. v.

or mātā-pitr̤i, s.m. Mother and father, parents; — lord, master, protector
To worship the goddess of small-pox
The days (or time) when small-pox rages
s.m. A small dome or building dedicated to the goddess of small-pox

def: s.m. The grain called kodoṅ (when it has an inebriating quality).
more: n. m. The grain kodoṅ , when it is intoxicating.

more: n. m. طبع stamped. A press ( chhāpe-khānā ).

s.m. per met. fr. matlab, corr. of مطلب mat̤lab, q.v. s.f. Mother ( = mā, māʼī, qq.v.)

more: (part. pass. of طبخ Heb. and Aram. ) adj. Cooked, dressed.

more: n. f. From H. mātā mother. A stepmother.

def: s.m. 1˚ = māṭ, 'earthen vessel,' q.v.; — 2˚ = maṭh, q.v. s.f. Mother ( = mā, māʼī, qq.v.)
adj. & s.m. = next, q.v.

s.m. A hut; the retired hut of an ascetic; a building inhabited by a devotee and his disciples; a monastery; college, school (especially for young Brāhmaṇs prosecuting sacred studies); — a pagan temple, a pagoda
subs: s.m. An abbot; a monk; an ascetic.

def: s. m. A Hindū college or convent, a pagan temple.
more: s. The fist, blows with the fist, clutch, grasp: see مشت musht. مٿهـ مرد muṭh-mard, s. m. A robber, a ruffian.
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit मठ (maṭha).

def: n. m. S. मठ् to dwell; Pr. maḍh'am . 1. A religious college or monastery. Bahut atīt maṭh kharābā . Prov. Where there are many Atīts the monastery is ruined? 2. A hut, esp. the retirement of an ascetic. maṭh-dhārī , n. m. An abbot; a monk. maṭh mārā jānā , v. n. To be marred, spoilt. maṭh mārnā , v. a. To mar; spoil.

ety: prob. Prk. मट्ठो; S. मृष्टः; cf. S. मार्ष and मारिषः
def: s.m. A species of pot-herb or vegetable, Amaranthus oleraceus, or A. tristis
s.m. The head; the forehead; the top or upper part (of anything); summit; — ridge (of thatch); prow (of a boat; syn. galahī)
more: n. m. S. मस्तक; Pr. mantham . 1. (Sant. molong ) The forehead. Kyā tumhāre hī mūthe ṭikā hai ? Prov. Saying. 2. The prow of a boat (E. galhī ). māthā bhaṛaknā , v. n. To feel headache. māthā piṭṭan k. , v. a. 1. ( Ironic .) To beat one's head in making a salām . 2. To labor for one. māthā ṭikānā , v. n. To prostrate oneself. māthā ṭhanaknā , Mah.; māthā ṭhinaknā , Hin.; māthe golī lagnā , Rus. v. n. lit . throbbing of the forehead. To feel a presentiment. Bhāiyoṅ kī sūṅ rī! us ne nigāhte hī mere māthe golī lāgī thī . Rus. I swear by my brothers! the moment I saw him I felt as if a ball had struck my head. (A presentiment of evil). māthā ragaṛnā , v. n. lit . to rub the forehead on the ground. To supplicate humbly. māthā godnā , v. a. To tattoo the forehead of a life prisoner or a slave. māthā mārnā , v. a. To expostulate or remonstrate with one in vain māthe rolī chāval chaṛhnā , v. a. T…

s. m. 1. ( मस्तक ) The forehead. 2. Prow. 3. ( मट्टक ) The ridge of thatch. ماتها ٿهنکنا māthā ṭhanaknā (lit. Ringing or throbbing of the forehead) implies a presentiment of the conclusion, from certain marks observed in the commencement of an affair: and, it is generally understood to indicate an unfortunate termination. ماتها رگڙنا māthā ragaṛnā (lit. To rub the forehead, viz. on the ground) To implore humbly (of the Deity, a saint or a king). ماتهي پر چڙهنا māthe par chaṛhnā, v. a. To tyrannise, to oppress.
s. (dakh.) Smallpox ( Harris ): see ماتا mātā, sign. 2.

more: s. m. Buttermilk. See مٿها maṭṭhā.

def: s.m. Stirring round; churning; — the tree Premna spinosa, or P. longifolia (the wood of which is used to produce fire by attrition)
more: n. f. Mind; intention. Bhāī! dhoī mānas kadhī mathan nāhīṅ de . Rus. My dear friend! rogues never reveal their secrets.
subs: (for sindhu-mathan). s.m. The churning of the ocean.

more: n. f. H. mathnā to churn. A churner (رئي).

def: s.f. A churn.

ety: for A. مطہرة mat̤harat, &c., n. of instr. fr. طہر 'to be or become clean or pure'
def: s.m. A vessel for purification, with which one washes himself, and performs the ablutions termed wuẓü; a ewer.
more: s. m. A vessel to hold water or to pour water from in purification.

ety: maṭhā = Prk. मट्ठअ = S. मृष्ट+क+ār = āl (caus. augment)+nā = Prk. अणअं = S. अनीयं
def: v.t. To hammer down the dints and inequalities of (a new metal vessel); to smooth and give shape by hammering to (a new metal vessel); to rub and beat (kneaded dough) with the knuckles; (fig.) to coax or wheedle, to talk over, win over by soft speech, &c., to prevail upon, to make willing; — to dilate or expatiate upon.
v. a. 1. To give a round shape to. Kaṭorā maṭhārnā . 2. See loch denā . 3. To talk prettily. 4. To dilate or expatiate upon. Dekho! kyā maṭhār maṭhār-ke bāteṅ banātā hai!
s.m. A sun-shade, parasol, parapluie, an umbrella (syn. sūraj-mukhī ćhātā).
more: s. m. A parasol, parapluie, umbrella.

ety: Prk. मट्टिओ+उल्ली and उल्लिआ; S. मृत्तिका+उल+ई and इका
def: s.f. A long (earthen) jar (for wine, oil, &c.).

s. m. Contribution, subscription, capitation.

s.f. A cell, cloister.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀫𑀼𑀝𑁆𑀞𑀺 (muṭṭhi) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀓- (-ka-), from Sanskrit मुष्टि (muṣṭi), from Proto-Indo-Iranian *muštíš (“fist”). Doublet of مُشْت (muśt). Cognate with Punjabi ਮੁੱਠ (muṭṭha), Bengali মুঠা (muṭha) and Persian مشت (mošt).

def: (see ماتها sign. 3.) v. a. To make even (particularly, a thatch).
s.f. corr. of maṭṭī, q.v. s.m. The evangelist Matthew; — the gospel according to St. Matthew.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀫𑀼𑀝𑁆𑀞𑀺 (muṭṭhi) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀓- (-ka-), from Sanskrit मुष्टि (muṣṭi), from Proto-Indo-Iranian *muštíš (“fist”). Doublet of مُشْت (muśt). Cognate with Punjabi ਮੁੱਠ (muṭṭha), Bengali মুঠা (muṭha) and Persian مشت (mošt).

def: s.m. Churning-stick, churn-staff; — the penis.
adj. See s.v. mat. s.f. Mother ( = mā, māʼī, qq.v.)
part. Stirred round, shaken about, agitated; churned; — s.m. (or f. in H.), Butter-milk without water, pure butter-milk.

ety: Prk. मथणअं; S. मन्थनीयं, rt. मन्थ or मथ्
def: v.t. To stir round; to churn; to beat or beat up; to knead, work, squeeze; to revolve (mentally), to ponder, consider; to agitate (a question), to discuss, debate; to sift, scrutinize, investigate.
more: v. a. S. मन्यन 1. To churn ( bilonā ). 2. To beat or beat up. Supne māṅ bhūk lagan lāgī, pīs bājrā ghāl kaṭhāle, Math math maiṅ povan lāgī . Rus. Song. I was so hungry in my dream, some grain I ground And kneaded it, and on the fire 'twas baking found. 3. To ponder over; revolve in one's mind.
1. v. a. To churn. 2. To knead, to work. 3. s. m. A churn-staff.

ety: S. मन्थन+इका; or fem. of mathnā
def: s.f. A churn-staff.

n. m. H. muṭṭhī fist. Onanism; masturbation; self-pollution. Laḍḍū meṅ, na peṛe meṅ, na barfī meṅ mazā hai, Jo mard-i-mujarrad ke maṭholoṅ meṅ mazā hai . Nazīr. maṭhol'iyā ; A. P. jalaq-bāz , n. m. Masturbator; manstrupator.

ety: Prk. मट्टिओ+उल्ली and उल्लिआ; S. मृत्तिका+उल+ई and इका
def: s.f. A long (earthen) jar (for wine, oil, &c.).
s.m. A buffoon; an ape (cf. māṭhā or maṭṭhā).
more: s. m. A buffoon. (A term of reproach).

def: n. m. S. मर्कट 1. A monkey. 2. A buffoon. Tum bhī nire māṭhū hī ho!
s.m. (lit. 'coming from or belonging to Mathura') An inhabitant (or a native) of Mathura; — name of a caste of Brāhmaṇs (syn. ćaube), and of a caste of Kāyaths.
more: n. m. 1. A class of Brahmans of Mathurā. 2. A class of Kāyasths.
s. m. 1. An inhabitant of Mathurā. 2. Name of a cast of Kāyaths; also, of Brāhmaṇs.

def: s.f. Name of a district and city in the province of Agra (it is held in great honour as the birth-place and early residence of Krishṇa, and is still important as a favourite resort of Hindū pilgrims)
more: s. f. A town in the province of Āgra, celebrated as the birthplace and early residence of Kr̤ish̤ṇa, and still an object of pilgrimage amongst Hindūs.
subs: (f. -in, or -ī), and mathurā-vāsī (f. -inī), s.m. An inhabitant of Mathurā.
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit मथुरा (mathurā).

def: s.m. Name of a caste of Brāhmaṇs of Mathurā; a man of that caste (syn. ćaube).
more: s. m. Name of a cast of Brāhmaṇs of Mathurā.

ety: fem. of mathuriyā, and = S. माथुर+इनी
def: s.f. A woman of the Mathuriyā caste; — the wife of a Mathuriyā.
more: s. f. The female of متهريا mathuriyā, q. v.

def: adj. (f. -ī), Dejected, sad; moping, sulky, in the dumps.
s.f. Earth, mould, soil; clay; dust, &c. (syn. maṭṭī or miṭṭī)
more: s. f. Earth: see مٿي miṭṭī. ماٿي پهکنا māṭī phaknā (dakh. i. q. حسد کرنا) To envy (?).
subs: 'To chuck dust or mud into the mouth'; to be consumed with envy, to be envious (of).
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀫𑀝𑁆𑀝𑀻 (maṭṭī), from Sanskrit मृत्ति॑का (mṛ́ttikā). Doublet of مِٹِّی (miṭṭī).

def: adj. fem. of mātā, q.v.
more: adj. 1. Full; blooming; glowing. 2. Intoxicated; drunk.

def: s.f. Mind, understanding, &c. (see mat)
more: n. f. S. मृत्तिका, Pālī, maṭṭikā . 1. Earth; clay. 1. Bore haiṅ miṭṭī ke! Delhi Street cry. 2. Māṭī oṛhnā, māṭī bichhonā, māṭī kā sirhānā re, Māṭī kā kalbūt banāyā, māṭī meṅ mil jānā re! Bhajan. Earth your garment, earth your bed, and pillow too, Earth-made image mingling with the earth anew. 3. Māṭī ho māṭī hone se pahle, maut ke āge maro rī! Holī. (Comp. Khāk shau pesh az āṅ ki khāk shavī . P.). In dust and ashes mourn before you dust become, die every day before death calls you to the tomb! 2. (Hin.) Flesh; meat; animal food. 3. A dead body; a corpse. 4. Dirt; filth; excrement. 5. Soil; land. 6. Sweepings; rubbish; trash. 7. Temperament; constitution. Ṭhanḍī miṭṭī hai. miṭṭī par laṛnā , v. a. To dispute about land. miṭṭī palīd, kharāb, khuār yā azīz h., miṭṭī bigaṛnā , v. n. 1. To be badly off. 1. Anjān kī miṭṭī kharāb hai . Prov. Saying. The stranger is always unfortunate. 2. Buṛhāpe…
subs: s.f., or mati-bhram, s.m. Confusion of mind, perplexity; misapprehension, error, mistake
adj. (f. -ā), Grave, sedate; collected, composed; resolved
adj. (f. -ā), Poor of understanding, of a barren or uninformed mind
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian متی (mattā), from Arabic مَتَّى (mattā). The shift from final -ā to -ī could be due to mispronunciation of the alif maqsura.

def: adj. (dialec.) = maṭiyā, q.v.
adj. (dialec.) = maṭiyālā, and maṭiyā, qq.v. adj. (f. -ī), Of earth or clay, clayey; made of mud; — earth-coloured, dusky, tawny.
adj. (f. -ī), Made of earth or clay, &c. ( = matiyā, and maṭiyālā, qq.v.); — good-for-nothing, worthless; idle, lazy.
adj. (f. -matī), Intelligent, clever, sensible, prudent, wise.
adj. Strong; firm; compact, solid; weighty, important; — obstinate; — vigorous, forcible; terse; sententious.
more: adj. 1. Vigorous; forcible; compact; sententious. 2. Solid; weighty; important. Matīn ādmī. matīn ibārat , n. f. A sententious style.
(from متن) adj. 1. Strong, firm, solid. 2. Obstinate. 3. Sententious.

ety: v.n. fr. مطر 'to descend with rapidity; — to rain,' &c.
def: adj. Raining; dropping; rained on.
more: (part. from مطر Heb. and Aram. ) adj. Rained on, raining.

def: n. m. A small water-melon.

def: s.m. A clay-pit.
n. m. A clay pit.

adj. 1. Clayey; made of mud. Ghar ghar yehī maṭyāle chūlhe . Prov. In every house an earthen fire place. (All alike). 2. Tawny; dusky. Maṭyālā raṅg .

def: adj. (f. -ī), Of earth or clay, clayey; made of mud; — earth-coloured, dusky, tawny.

ety: maṭṭī, q.v.+ā = āw = Prk. आव or आवे = S. आपि (caus. augment)+nā = Prk. अणअं = S. अनीयं
def: v.t. To rub or scrub with earth or mud; — to plaster or smear with mud; — v.n. To be reduced to dust; to be levelled or razed; to be or become extinguished
more: v. n. To wink at, to connive, to suffer, to tolerate.

ety: maṭī˚, prob. akin to S. मृष्; ˚ānā, as in the preceding, q.v.; — cf. also maṭaknā
def: v.t. To wink at, connive at; to suffer, tolerate; — to pretend not to hear; to turn a deaf ear to; to make no answer.

v. a. 1. To scrub with. Hāth maṭyā to! 2. To plaster or besmear with mud. maṭiyā'nā ; Bhoj. maṭiyāval , v. n. 1. To pretend not to hear; turn a deaf ear to; make no answer. 2. To connive at. 3. To suffer; tolerate. 4. To be extinguished.
s.m. Connivance; sufferance, toleration; turning a deaf ear to, &c.

ety: maṭṭī, q.v.+Prk. आलो, and आलओ; S. आलः, and आल+कः
def: adj. & s.m. Made, or consisting, of mud or clay, clayey ( = maṭiyālā); — s.m. Clayey soil, rich soil; arable land. (N.B. The subst. maṭiyār is sometimes fem.).
n. f. Clayey soil (first quality).

ety: maṭṭī, q.v.+Prk. आलो, and आलओ; S. आलः, and आल+कः
def: adj. & s.m. Made, or consisting, of mud or clay, clayey ( = maṭiyālā); — s.m. Clayey soil, rich soil; arable land. (N.B. The subst. maṭiyār is sometimes fem.).
more: s. m. Arable land, rich soil.

def: v.t. (dialec.) = maṭiyānā, q.v. v.t. To rub or scrub with earth or mud; — to plaster or smear with mud; — v.n. To be reduced to dust; to be levelled or razed; to be or become extinguished
s.m. (f. -ī), A large earthen jar or pot (cf. māṭ).
more: s. m. A large earthen jar or pot.

def: s.m. = maṭkanā, q.v. v.t. To roll or move about (the eyes), to wink (the eye); to wanton with (the eyes); to ogle; — to shake (a limb or member, or the clothes, &c.); to work (the hips); to make a display before.
v. n. An earthen mug (کلهرَا).
s.f. Winking; ogling; mincing; coquetry (syn. maṭak).

ety: maṭkā, q.v.+nā = lā = Prk. डओ = S. र or ल+कः
def: s.m. A small earthen vessel or mug (such as is used for taking water out of a larger).
v. a. 1. To wink; ogle; wanton with one's eyes. Dekho kaisī āṅkheṅ maṭkātā hai! 2. To work one's hips. 3. To make a display. Sab se pahle tum hī kapṛe maṭkā-kar ho baiṭhe .!

ety: caus. of maṭaknā; and = maṭak˚+ānā, as in miṭānā, q.v.
def: v.t. To roll or move about (the eyes), to wink (the eye); to wanton with (the eyes); to ogle; — to shake (a limb or member, or the clothes, &c.); to work (the hips); to make a display before.
more: (causal of مٿکنا) v. a. To wink, to ogle, to twinkle.

ety: prob. for मुटकी muṭkī = mūṭh+Prk. अक+इआ = S. अ+क+इका
def: s.f. A plaything which infants suck; a child's coral.
wety: Feminine of مَٹْکا (maṭkā)

def: s.f. A wink; — a twinkle; closing the eyes.
wety: Feminine of مَٹْکا (maṭkā)

def: s.f. A small earthen pot or jar; a vessel in which milk is churned (dim. of maṭkā, q.v.; cf. maṭkanā).
more: s. f. 1. A childʼs coral, or plaything which infants suck. 2. A small earthen pot or jar. 3. A wink, a twinkle, closing the eyes.
wety: Feminine of مَٹْکا (maṭkā)

def: s.m. A house of mud or earth.
more: s. m. A house of earth.

ety: n. of place fr. طلع 'to rise,' &c.
def: s.m. The place of rising (of the stars, or of the sun); the east; — the beginning or exordium (of a qaṣīda or ode)
more: n. m. طلع 1. The place of rising; the east. 2. The beginning or exordium of a poem. matlٴa sāf h. , v. n. 1. To be clear (the horizon). 2. To be clear of obstacles, etc.
(noun of place of طلع) s. m. 1. The place of rising, the rising (of the sun, &c.), the east. 2. The beginning or exordium of a poem. Plur. مطلعات Openings of poems.
(part. act. VIII. of طلع) adj. Perceiving, inspecting, beholding, regarding, knowing, informed, acquainted.
subs: The sky, or the horizon, to be clear; (fig.) obstacles to be removed; the coast to be clear
The part of the sky (where the new moon will be visible) is clear; — (fig.) the coast is clear, there is no obstacle in the way.

ety: inf. n. of طلب 'to seek, desire, dedemand,' &c.
def: s.m. A question, demand, request, petition; proposition; wish, desire; object, intention, aim, purpose, pursuit, motive; — adv. To come to (one's) object; in short
more: n. m. طلب See ارتهـ 1, 2, 7, and 8. Dunyā hai, aur matlab . Prov. The world is all for self. matlab-barārī , n. f. The discharge or performance of a work. matlab-barārī k., matlab bar lānā , v. n. To give effect to; bring about. matlab rakhnā . See garaz rakhnā. matlab kā yār yā garzī, matlabī, matlabiyā , adj. See garaz kā yār. matlab ko pahuṅchānā , To effect; accomplish. matlab kī ghāt chalnā , v. n. To look to one's own interest. Dugānā jān! maiṅ matlab kī ghāt chaltī hūṅ, Khilāë pān to angiyā karūṅ rafū terī! Wom. Rāhat My bed-fellow, my life! my interest I pursue, You give me pan , your boddice I will darn for you! matlab nikālnā , v. a. See garaz nikālnā. matlab h. , v. n. 1. To gain one's end. 2. To be all up with one. Bhūk ke māre matlab ho gayā. matlab yeh ki , See garaz yeh ki .
(from طلب) s. m. A question, petition, intention, wish, desire, object, aim, purpose, purport, meaning, demand; in short.
subs: s.f. The discharge or performance of a work; the accomplishment of an object, attainment of an end
or mat̤lab bar-lānā (apnā), To give effect to (one's) wish or intention; to accomplish, or bring about (one's) object, or end
To have some motive; to be interested, &c. (syn. garaẓ rakhnā)
s.m. A friend for his own interests, or through interested motives; a time-serving, or a selfish, friend
To accomplish (one's) object, to gain (one's) end, to get what one wanted
To pursue what is for one's own interest, or to look to one's own interest
(-se), To effect one's purpose (by or through), to make what use one desired (of)
A desire to be accomplished, an end to be gained
To be all up (with one), to be reduced to the last extremity
The purport is this; in short, in brief.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مَطْلَب (matlab), borrowed from Arabic مَطْلَب (maṭlab), from طَلَبَ (ṭalaba). First attested in c. 1635 as Middle Hindi مطلب (mt̤lb /⁠matlab⁠/).

def: s.m. corr. of مطلب mat̤lab, q.v. s.f. Mother ( = mā, māʼī, qq.v.)
adj. & s.m. Having an object or a purpose to serve, interested; selfish, self-seeking; — an interested person, &c.
wety: From مَطْلَب (matlab, “purpose”) + ـی (-ī).

def: adj. & s.m. Having an object or a purpose to serve, interested; selfish, self-seeking; — an interested person, &c.
s.f. Nausea.
s.f. Nausea.

ety: mat˚ = S. मथ्य(ते), pass. of rt. मथ्+lā caus. aff.; cf. matnā = mathnā
def: v.n. To be sick (at the stomach), to feel nausea (syn. maćlānā).
more: Sant. v. n. To be sick; to feel nausea.

def: v. n. To be sick at the stomach, to feel nausea.
adj. (dakh.) Loquacious ( Harris ).
s.f. Nausea, inclination to vomit
more: n. f. Nausea. matlī h. , v. n. To feel nausea.

ety: pass. part. of طلب 'to seek, request, demand,' &c.
def: part. adj. Sought, required, demanded, desired, longed for; wanted, needed, necessary; — s.m. A quest; a desire; an object, a purpose.
more: adj. 1. Desired; sought. 2. Demanded; called for.
(part. pass. of طلب) adj. Demanded, required, wanted, desired, necessary. s. m. Object, purpose, desire.

ety: for A. مطلوبة mat̤lūbat, fem. of mat̤lūb
def: part. adj. Sought, required, demanded, desired, longed for; wanted, needed, necessary; — s.m. A quest; a desire; an object, a purpose.
s.m. Text (of a book); — middle (of a road, or book, or piece of cloth, &c.).
more: n. m. 1. The middle. 2. The text. Kitāb, yā dushāle kā matan .
(Heb. and Aram. ) s. m. The text, the middle.

ety: māt˚ = Prk. मत्त(इ), fr. S. मत्त, p.p.p. fr. rt. मद्
def: v.n. To be intoxicated or drunk; — to be puffed up (with pride).
more: E. v. n. To be intoxicated.

ety: mat˚ = Prk. मंत(इ), or मंते(इ), fr. S. मन्त्र
def: v.n. To hold consultation (with, -se), to consult together, to consult; to agree together.
more: v. n. S. मृष्ट wiped. 1. To be expunged, erased (اتَهنا 31). 2. To be destroyed, annihilated; to come to naught. 3. To come to an end; to become extinct. 4. To die out ( uṭhnā , 47). 5. To be abolished, nullified, cancelled. 6. To be madly in love with. Yeh bhī un par miṭe baiṭhe haiṅ .
s. m. A kind of sugar-cane.
(see متهنا mathnā ) v. a. (dakh.) To mash, to mix together grain.

def: s.m. A kind of sugar-cane.

v.t. corr. of mathnā, q.v. v.t. To stir round; to churn; to beat or beat up; to knead, work, squeeze; to revolve (mentally), to ponder, consider; to agitate (a question), to discuss, debate; to sift, scrutinize, investigate.
s.f. What is relied on (in a suit at law); documents.

ety: Prk. मट्टिआ + उल्लओ; S. मृत्तिका+उल+कः; cf. maṭ = maṭṭī
def: s.m. A mud-heap (syn. tūda); a mound or pillar of earth raised to mark a station (or the position of a flag) of a survey; a boundary-pillar (of mud).

s.f. Measure; quantity; a minute portion, particle, atom, a little, trifle; an element; an ingredient; an instant; a dose (of medicine); — a prosodial instant, the unit (in metre), the length of time required to pronounce a short syllable; — materials, property, goods, possessions, wealth, substance, means of subsistence; — a vowel-symbol; the upper or horizontal limb of the Nāgarī characters
more: n. f. 1. A Hindī vowel or diacritical mark. 2. A dose of medicine. 3. Property; wealth. mātrā lagānā , v. a. To put in the vowel marks.
s. f. 1. A vowel. 2. A dose, an ingredient.

def: s.m. Measure, quantity; — a little, trifle, somewhat; an atom; element; a moment; — a short vowel; — totality, the whole; — adj. & adv. (as last member of compounds), Little; mere; in the measure or amount of, so much as; only, merely, nothing but, solely, wholly; even; just (e.g. ćhin-mātr, 'as much as a moment, only a moment'; manushya-mātr, 'only a man, a mere man').
more: ( In comp .) In the measure or amount of, as chhin-mātr .
wety: From Sanskrit.

def: adj. & adv. Of the measure or size (of); as much as; mere; merely, only, solely, &c. (used only as last member of compounds; see mātra).
adj. (f. -ī), Mixed with peas; — s.m. Barley mixed with peas.

ety: fr. S. मन्त्र; or prob. caus. of matnā, q.v.
def: v.t. To persuade.

s.f. A mother; — a respectful term used in addressing women, near relatives, and elderly women generally; an epithet of Lakshmī, and of Durgā; — a divine mother, a personined energy of a deity or of his wife; — the wife of a Brāhmaṇ; — a cow; — the earth; — space, ether
subs: or mātr̤i-bandhu, s.m.f. A relation by the mother's side, maternal relation
s.f. The honour or reputation of a mother
s.m. That which is given by a mother to her daughter at her marriage
or mātr̤i-ghātī, or mātr̤i-ghna, or mātr̤i-hā, or mātr̤i-han, s.m. The murderer of his own mother, a matricide.

def: adj. & adv. = mātra (at the end of compounds); — 2˚ adj. Of the nature of matter, material; containing a short vowel.
s.f. A prosodial instant; a simple sound.
s.f. A mother; — a divine mother, epithet of a class of goddesses; — a nurse; — the alphabet.
adj. (f. -ī), Mixed with peas ( = maṭrālā).

ety: pass. part. of طرح 'to cast, throw; to remove far away, to reject,' &c.
def: part. adj. Thrown, cast; removed far away, distant; — rejected; dejected; abject; — s.m. An infidel; — (in subtraction) a remainder.
more: (part. pass. of طرح Heb. and Aram. ) 1. adj. Rejected, dejected, abject. 2. (subst.) An infidel. (Plur. مطروحات).

def: s.m. pl. (of mat̤rūḥ), Things thrown away or rejected.

ety: pass. part. of ترك 'to leave, forsake,' &c.
def: part. Left, relinquished, forsaken, abandoned; rejected, abolished; omitted, neglected; obsolete, fallen into disuse
more: adj. ترک quitted. 1. Left; relinquished; abandoned; rejected.

(part. pass. of ترک Chald. ) adj. Abolished, obsolete, rejected, relinquished. مال متروک māl-i-matrūk, Estate of a person deceased. متروک الاستعمال matrūku-l-istiﻌmāl or متروک رسم matrūk-i-rasm, Unusual, not practised, disused, obsolete.
subs: adj. Not practised, disused, unusual, obsolete
s.m. Estate of a person deceased.

adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Envious, jealous; hostile; grudging; selfish; covetous, greedy, niggardly; — an envious man; a niggard, &c.; — envy, jealousy; enmity, hostility; — anger, passion; — greediness, selfish eagerness.
s.f. An envious woman; — a selfish or greedy woman, &c.; — impatience of another's success or prosperity, envy, jealousy; enmity, hostility; passion, anger.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Selfish; impatient of another's prosperity, envious, jealous; stupid.
adj. (f. -iṇī), Envious, &c. ( = matsar, q.v.); — wicked, depraved, bad; — s.m. An envious man; a niggard, &c.
s.m. Selfishness; envy, jealousy; malice, spite; — displeasure, dissatisfaction.

s.m. (f. -ī), A fish (syn. maććhī); — name of the first of the ten incarnations or manifestations of Vishṇu (see Williams' Sanskrit Dict., s.v. matsya); — name of one of the Purāṇas; and of a country (Dinājpur and Rangpur) enumerated among the midland divisions of India
subs: (˚ya+av˚), s.m. 'Fish-descent,' name of the first incarnation of Vishṇu
s.m. 'Fish-king,' the rohū, Cyprinus rohita; — the country called Matsya (see above).
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit मत्स्य (mátsya).

ety: Prk. मत्तो; S. मत्तः, rt. मद्
def: part. adj. (f. -ā), Intoxicated (by liquor, or passion, or pride, &c.); overpowered (by sleep, &c.); maddened, mad, furious; — pleased, delighted; — s.m. A drunken person; a madman; — an elephant in rut, a furious elephant.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀫𑀁𑀢 (maṃta)), an extension of Ashokan Prakrit 𑀫𑀸 (mā), from Sanskrit मा (mā́), from Proto-Indo-Iranian *máH, from Proto-Indo-European *méh₁.

def: s.f. (of matta), An intoxicated woman; — a furious female elephant; — any intoxicating drink, spirituous or vinous liquor.
s.m. (dialec.) A bough of the tāṛ or Borassus flabelliformis; or of the cocoa-nut palm, Cocos nucifera; a club, a bat.
s.m. (mattatā, f.) Intoxication; excitement of the passions; infatuation; madness.
adj. & adv. Drunken, intoxicated; — like one intoxicated; in the manner of a drunkard.

ety: Prk. मट्ठअं; S. मृष्ट+कं; or S. मथित+कं
def: s.m. Butter-milk; 'tyre' with an admixture of water; (dialec.) milk coagulated with its butter in it and churned; — the coagulum or thick residue of curds.
more: s. m. ( मंथित ) Buttermilk. ¶ miṭṭhā (or dakh. miṭhā ) 1. (perhaps, from मिष्ठ Sweet) s. m. A kiss. 2. ( मिष्ठ ) adj. Sweet: slow. See ميٿها mīṭhā.

ety: Prk. मिट्ठओ; S. मिष्ट+कः; cf. mīṭhā; — or Prk. मट्ठओ; S. मृष्ट+कः
def: adj. (f. -ī), Slow, sluggish, slack; dull, heavy, stolid; — perverse, obstinate; — rubbed or worn smooth; smooth (as a file, &c.).

ety: maṭṭhā+pan = Prk. प्पणं = S. त्वं (Vedic त्वनं)
def: s.m. Slowness, sluggishness, slackness; dulness, heaviness, stolidity; — perverseness, obstinacy.
n. f. 1. Slowness; slackness ( sustī-pan , 1). 2. Dullness; stolidity. maṭṭhā sohan . ( Mech .) A smooth file.

ety: maṭṭh(ā), q.v.+ar = Prk. अडो = S. अ+रः; cf. S. मन्थरः
def: adj. Slow, &c. ( = maṭṭhā, q.v.). s.m. Butter-milk; 'tyre' with an admixture of water; (dialec.) milk coagulated with its butter in it and churned; — the coagulum or thick residue of curds.
s.m. The evangelist Matthew; — the gospel according to St. Matthew.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian متی (mattā), from Arabic مَتَّى (mattā). The shift from final -ā to -ī could be due to mispronunciation of the alif maqsura.

def: adj. Made or composed of earth; earthy; mixed with earth (as metal in the ore); — frail, fragile; infirm, feeble; — s.m. A well without a frame (i.e. a well merely excavated), a kaććā well

adj. & s.m. Idle, lazy; — a lazy fellow
s.m. A kind of snake with earth-like or dark spots
s.m. A tenement of earth or clay.

def: s.f. (rustic or dialec.) Mother ( = māʼī, mātā)
subs: (S. mātr̤i-gr̤iham), s.m. Mother's house or family; maternal relations.

more: ( Gram .) The first of two words or clauses connected by a conjunction. mātūf-alaih , ( Gram .) The second word or clause joined by a conjunction.
(part. pass. of عطف) adj. Inclined, turned; conjoined (in grammar).

ety: for A. معطوفة maʻt̤ūfat, fem. of maʻt̤ūf

s.m. pl. (of mat̤ʻūmat, fem. of mat̤ʻūm), Eatables, victuals, viands.
more: s. plur. (part. pass. of طعام) Victuals, eatables, viands.

ety: pass. part. of طعن 'to pierce, stab,' &c. (lit. & fig.

more: (part. pass. of طعن Heb. and Aram. ) adj. Reproached, chidden.

def: adj. (f. -ī), & s.m. Intoxicated, drunken, drunk; — a drunken man
more: adj. Intoxicated; drunken; drunk. 1. Jījā ke māl par sālī matvālī . Prov. The brother-in-law hath riches great, the sister-in-law's intoxicate. 2. Sāt hāth hāthī se rahiye, pāṅch hāth saṅghāre se, Bīs hāth nārī se rahye, tīs hāth matvāre se! Prov. From elephant six cubits, and from ox's horns five keep, From woman twenty keep, the drunkard give thrice ten arms' sweep! matvālā h. , v. n. To be drunk.
(Sans. मत्त Drunk, and Hind. والا) adj. Intoxicated, drunken, drunk.
subs: or ho-jānā, v.n. To be or become drunk.

ety: matwāl = matwālā+pan = Prk. प्पणं = S. त्वं (Vedic त्वनं)

adj. = matwālā, q.v. adj. (f. -ī), & s.m. Intoxicated, drunken, drunk; — a drunken man

s.m. Stupidity, want of understanding, ignorance, folly, fatuity; — spiritual folly.

s.m. (dialec.) = مہوا mahūʼā, q.v. s.m. The tree Bassia latifolia, and its flower (a spirituous liquor is distilled from the blossom; and oil is extracted from the nuts or seeds, which is used instead of butter, and to adulterate ghī)
subs: s.m. Oil extracted from the seed of the mahuʼā
Spirituous liquor distilled from the flower of the mahuʼā.

def: adj. & adv. Single; — singly, separately, one at a time.
more: n. m. A unitarian, as all Vedāntis and Mahomedans are.
(part. act. II. of أحد and وحد Chald. ) adj. Believing in one God, orthodox. ¶ mauḥad, Single.

def: s.m. A gift, a present.
more: (from وهب Heb. and Aram. ) s. m. A present, a gift.

def: s.f. = mauhib, q.v. s.m. A gift, a present.
more: s. A gift: see موهب mauhib.

def: part. Given, presented, conferred
subs: or mauhūb la-hu, One to whom a gift is made, or on whom something is bestowed; a legatee.

def: (pass. part. of وهم 'to think, imagine,' &c.), part. adj, Thought, imagined, fancied; supposed, surmised; — imaginary; ideal.
more: (part. pass. of وهم) adj. Imagined, imaginary.

def: adj. 1. Imagined. 2. Imaginary; ideal.
s.m. Promising; a promise; — auguring, prognosticating; predicting, foretelling.
more: (inf. and noun of place or time from وعد Heb. and Aram. and ) s. m. 1. A promise. 2. Predicting, foretelling.

ety: for A. موعظة, inf. n. of وعظ 'to admonish,' &c.
def: s.f. Admonishing, exhorting; admonition; exhortation; advice.
more: (from وعظ Heb. and Aram. ) s. f. An advice, exhortation.

def: s.f. A wave, surge, billow (syn. lahar); — whim, caprice; — emotion, ecstasy; — heaps, abundance, plenty
more: n. f. 1. A wave ( lahr ). 2. Emotion; extasy. 3. Abundance; plenty. Barse Āsauj to ho nāj kī mauj . Prov. In August if it rain great heaps there 'll be of grain. mauj ānā , To be transported, moved, thrilled. mauj-zan , adj. Beating high (waves). mauj k., yā mārnā , v. n. To enjoy oneself. mauj mārnā , v. n. To beat high (waves).

s. f. 1. A wave, surge. 2. Whim, caprice. موج آجاني mauj ā-jānī, An imagination to come into the mind. موج خيز mauj-ḵẖez, adj. Stormy, producing waves. موج رکهنا mauj rakhnā, To be proud. موج زن mauj-zan, Waving, wavy, boisterous, billowy, tumultuous, agitated, raging (sea). موج زني mauj-zan-ī, s. f. Waving, billowing. موج مارنا mauj mārnā, v. a. To billow, to wave; ( met. ) is applied to express self enjoyment without restraint. Muhammad Baḳā says: سيل سرشک اپنا جب سرباوج ماري طوفان نوح بيٿها گوشي مين موج ماري Sail-i-sarashk apnā jab sar ba-auj māre, t̤ūfān-i-Nūḥ baiṭhā goshe meṅ mauj māre. When the deluge of my tears rises to its height, that of Noah may amuse itself in a corner. موج نسيم mauj-i-nasīm, A cooling squall.
subs: v.n. An idea or whim to come (into the mind); — to be transported, or moved, or thrilled
v.n. To be whimsical; to be pround
adj. Of which the waves beat high; waving, wavy, billowy, agitated, raging (sea), boisterous, tumultuous
or mauj mārnā, v.n. To biliow, wave, beat high (the sea); — to fluctuate, to waver; to be capricious; to enjoy oneself without restraint
To rise in billows, to surge (the sea, &c.); — to get into a state of ecstasy
wety: Borrowed from Arabic مَوْج (mawj).

ety: for A. موجة maujat, n. of un. fr. mauj
def: s.m. A wave, &c. (i.q. mauj, q.v.). s.f. A wave, surge, billow (syn. lahar); — whim, caprice; — emotion, ecstasy; — heaps, abundance, plenty
more: s. m. A wave, billow, surge.
subs: v.n. An idea or whim to come (into the mind); — to be transported, or moved, or thrilled
v.n. To be whimsical; to be pround
adj. Of which the waves beat high; waving, wavy, billowy, agitated, raging (sea), boisterous, tumultuous
or mauj mārnā, v.n. To biliow, wave, beat high (the sea); — to fluctuate, to waver; to be capricious; to enjoy oneself without restraint
To rise in billows, to surge (the sea, &c.); — to get into a state of ecstasy

def: adj. Whimsical; — emotional (i.q. lahrī, q.v.). adj. & s.m. Fanciful, whimsical, capricious, fitful, inconstant, fickle; emotional; — merry, gay, jovial; — odd, fantastic; — careless, inconsiderate, thoughtless; — a fanciful (or an emotional) person, &c.; a merry-andrew, a clown.

ety: pass. part. of وجد 'to be found, to exist,' &c.
def: part. adj. Found; — brought into existence; existing; extant; — present; standing before; ready, at hand; available
more: adj. وجد existed. See حاضر (1, 2). 1. Present. 2. At hand; available. 3. Existing; extant. 4. Ready. maujūd rahnā , v. n. 1. To subsist; continue. 2. To attend; wait on. maujūd k. , v. a. 1. To produce; bring before ( hāzir k .). 2. To provide; supply.
(part. pass. of وجد) adj. Found, present, existing, existant, standing before, at hand, ready. Plur. موجودات maujūdāt, s. f. Things found or present, creatures, existences, beings, effects, assets.
subs: To exist; to subsist; to continue; — to be present; to attend, to wait (on)
v.t. To make present, to bring before, to produce; — to provide, supply.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مَوْجُود (mawjūd), itself from Arabic مَوْجُود (mawjūd), from the Arabic root و ج د (w j d).

def: (for A. موجودة maujūdat, fem. of maujūd), part. adj. f. = maujūd, q.v.
subs: Existing, or present, state or circumstances.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian موجوده (mawjūda), from Arabic مَوْجُودَة (mawjūda), feminine of مَوْجُود (mawjūd).

def: s.f. Existence; presence
more: n. f. Presence; existence. maujūdgī meṅ , adv. In presence of. maujūd'ah , adj. 1. Present. 2. Extant. hālat-i-maujūdah . The present state or circumstances.
subs: In the presence (of, -kī); during the existence (of).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian موجودگی (mawjūdagī). Equivalent of مَوجُودَہ (maujūda, “present, attendant”) + ـگی (-gī, “-ance, -y”).

r: maujūdāt, maujūd, maujūdat, maujūdāt lenā, kī
def: s.f. pl. (of maujūd; or of maujūdat, fem. of maujūd), Existing things, existences; created beings, creatures, beings; nature; — things found; things present; — effects, assets; ready money, cash, specie; — a review, or muster (of troops)
more: n. f. 1. Existing things; created beings; nature. 2. A muster of troops. maujūdāt lenā , v. n. 1. To muster (troops, etc.). 2. To check; audit.
subs: (-kī), To take the muster (of), to muster; — to make the roll-call (of); — to check, to audit.

ety: prop. mauqěʻ, n. of place and time fr. وقع 'to fall; to happen, occur,' &c.
def: s.m. A place where anything falls, or happens; — a fit place; — place, situation, locality; — fit or proper time, opportunity, occasion; — occurrence; accident, contingency; — adj. Opportune; proper, fit, suitable
more: n. m. 1. Locality; place; situation. 2. Opportunity; occasion; proper time. mauqe par , adj. At the proper time; at the right place and time. mauqā pakaṛnā, mauqe ko hāth se na denā . To make the best of one's time; not to let slip an opportunity; to take time by the forelock. mauqa dekhnā yā taknā . To look for an opportunity. mauqā denā , v. a. To give one an opportunity. mauqe se , adv. Timely; opportunely. mauqe kī , adj. Fitting; appropriate. mauqā milnā, bannā, lagnā, yā hāth lagnā , v. n. To get an opportunity. mauqā nikal jānā , v. n. To lose one's opportunity. mauqa-i-vārdāt . G. G. The place where a crime has been committed. be-mauqa , adj. 1. Misplaced; out of place. 2. Untimely; unseasonable; premature. 3. Ill-timed; inopportune; mal-a-propos. be-mauqa, yā mauqa be-mauqā bolnā , v. n. To speak at the wrong time.
(noun of place of وقع) s. m. A place where any thing happens, a fit place, a place, situation; a contingency.
subs: An opportunity to occur, or to present itself
adv. In the right place; at the proper time; on occasion
To seize the occasion; not to let slip an opportunity
or mauqaʻ taknā (-kā), To look, or watch for, an opportunity
(-ko), To give an opportunity, or occasion (to)
adj. (f. -kī), Opportune; proper, appropriate, fit, suitable
or mauqaʻ lagnā (-ko), An opportunity to present itself (to); to meet with, or to get, an opportunity
An opportunity to pass away, or escape, or be lost
s.m. The place where a crime has been committed
(-ke), An opportunity to come to hand, &c. (i.q. mauqaʻ milnā, q.v.)
adj. & adv. Out of place, misplaced; — untimely, unseasonable; ill-timed, inopportune; mal-à-propos, inconvenient, untoward; — at the wrong time; inopportunely, &c.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian موقع (mauqi', mauqa', “time”), from Arabic مَوْقِع (mawqiʕ).

def: s.m. Precedence; pre-eminence; chiefship.

ety: n. of place fr. وکب 'to be assiduous,' &c.
def: s.m. An army, forces; a large detachment (either of horse or foot); a choice body of troops; a body-guard, life-guards; — a large cavalcade.
more: (noun of place from وکب) s. m. An army, forces, a choice body of troops.

ety: n. of place fr. وقف 'to stand; to stop, stay,' &c.
def: s.m. A place where one remains; a station, post, place.
more: (noun of place of وقف s. m. A station, post, place.

def: part. adj. Stopped; settled; rested; — delayed, deferred, postponed; suspended; relinquished; abolished; dismissed; discontinued; ceased; — fixed; bound; supported; established; determined; — belonging, or restricted (to), dependent (upon, -par); — left or bequeathed for pious purposes; — s.m. A legacy, a bequest; a grant of land, &c. (generally for charitable or pious purposes)
more: adj. وقف stopped. 1. Stopped; ceased; abolished. 2. Dependent on ( munhasar ). Yeh bāt unhīṅ hī par mauqūf hai . 3. Dismissed. 4. Abolished. mauqūf-ilaihi . A grantee. mauqūf rakhnā , v. a. To suspend ( multavī rakhnā ). mauqūf-ٴalah . A referee. mauqūf k. , v. a. 1. To discontinue; stop. 2. To leave off; lay or put aside. 3. To remove from office ( javāb denā ). mauqūf h., yā mauqūfī meṅ ānā , v. n. 1. To be discontinued; stopped. 2. To be dismissed; discharged.

(part. pass. of وقف) adj. Stopped, settled, rested, bound, fixed or dependant (on), ceased, deferred, postponed, abolished, relinquished. موقوف کرنا mauḳūf karnā, To stop, cease, leave off, lay aside, abolish, abrogate, annul. موقوف هونا mauḳūf honā, To depend or rest (on), to be stopped, to cease.
subs: v.t. To keep in abeyance; to suspend; — to keep, or make, dependent (on, -par)
adj. Paused upon; — dependent (upon); — s.m. A word paused on; — a referee
v.t. To stop, to cause to cease, to discontinue, to leave off; to lay or put aside; to postpone, defer; to abolish, abrogate, annul; to remove (from office), dismiss, discharge
v.n. To be stopped, be discontinued; to stop, cease; — to be dismissed (from office)

def: s.f. Stopping, stoppage, cessation, discontinuance; state of abeyance; suspension, arrest (as of judgment in a law-court); — the setting aside; dismissal (from office)
subs: To come under dismissal, to be dismissed (from office).

def: v.n. ( = walyuṉ), fr. وَلِيَ 'to be near,' &c.), s.m. Lord, master, ruler; a patron; a judge; magistrate; the Supreme Lord; — a slave, servant (syn. g̠ǒlām); a manumitted slave, freedman
more: n. m. Master; lord; God. 1. Jidhar maulā, udhar Āsf-ud-daulā . Prov. 2. Marzī-i-maulā az hamah aulā . P. Prov. God's will above all things. maulā-daulā , An open-handed, liberal man. Woh to maulā daulā haiṅ. maulā Alī . A form of salutation among Mah. faqirs. maulā ke nām , In the name of God.
(for موليٰ from ولي) s. m. 1. A judge, magistrate, master, lord, the lord, God. 2. A slave.
subs: s.m. A benefactor, helper; an open-handed liberal man
'The lord ʻAlī'; a form of salutation among faqīrs (cf. H. rām-rām)
adv. In the name, or for the sake or cause, of God
'Our lord'; my lord judge; a title given to judges, prefects, &c., and to persons respected for learning; a learned doctor.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مَوْلَا (mawlā), from Arabic مَوْلًى (mawlan).

def: s.m. See مولا maulā.
more: (from ولي) s. m. See مولا maulā.

def: adj. Proceeding from a root; radical; original; — handed down from antiquity; ancient, of old standing; descending from those who were resident in a place when it was built; — of pure blood, of good extraction or stock, high-born.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀫𑀽𑀮 (mūla), from Sanskrit मूल (mūla). Cognate with Bengali মূল (mul), Gujarati મૂળ (mūḷ).

def: s.f. Lordship; judgeship.
s. f. Judgeship, lordship.
v.n. = maurānā, and maulnā, qq.v. v.n. (dialec.) To blossom; to flourish (i.q. maulnā).
more: (lit. our lord or judge) s. m. A title given to persons respected for learning.

def: s.m. (dialec.) contrac. of next, q.v.
s.m. The maternal uncles of the husband and wife as related to one another.
more: Mah. n. m. maulas , Hin.; mamiyā sās , Mah. n. f. The maternal uncles of the husband and wife as related to one another.

def: s.m. A Muḥammadan doctor of law; a professor; a learned man.
more: n. m. A Mah. professor or doctor.
(from موليٰٰ) s. m. A learned man.

def: v.n. = maurānā, and maulnā, qq.v. v.n. (dialec.) To blossom; to flourish (i.q. maulnā).
adj. (f. -ī), Of or belonging or related to, or coming from, a maternal uncle
subs: s.m. The son of a maternal uncle, a cousin
s.f. Maternal uncle's daughter, a cousin.

def: s. m. (dakh.) Motherʼs brotherʼs son, cousin.
s. f. (dakh.) Motherʼs brotherʼs daughter.
s.f. The earth.

s.f. (dialec.) Mother, &c. (i.q. māʼī, q.v.). s.f. Mother, &c. ( = mā, q.v.); — a woman's breast. s.m. (in Prosody) A molossus or metrical foot of three long syllables; — a magical or mystical formula; a name of Brahmā, of Vishṇu, and of Śiva.

s.m. & f. (the form maulī is gen. fem.), A crown, diadem, tiara; a crest; a tuft; a lock of hair left on the crown of the head after tonsure, a top-knot (syn. ćūḍā); — the head; — a red thread (syn. nārā).

ety: inf. n., and n. of place, fr. ولد 'to give birth to,' &c.
def: s.m. Birth, nativity; — birth-place; — time of birth.
more: (inf. or noun of place of ولد Heb. and Aram. ) s. m. Nativity, birth, place of birth, birthplace.

def: adj. Of low origin; of inferior rank or caste (among Kāyasths).
more: adj. Of inferiour rank or cast among Kāyasths. (Beng. from Sans. मौलिक ).

ety: prob. fr. maul = maur, 'blossom,' q.v.; — or maul˚ = Prk. मोल(इ) or मोले(इ) = S. मौलय(ति), denom, v. fr. मौल
def: v.n. To blossom, bud, bloom; to flourish; — v.t. To exhilarate; to intoxicate (said of bhang).
more: v. n. 1. To bloom, to bud, to blossom (the mango tree). 2. To intoxicate (as, bhang, &c.).

ety: maul˚ = mauṛ, q.v.+rā = arā = Prk. अडओ = S. र+कः
def: s.m. = mauṛ, q.v. s.m. A bud; blossom.

ety: mauṛ, q.v. + rī = arī = Prk. अडिआ = S. र+इका
def: s.f. = mauṛī, q.v.
s.f. The tree Mimusops elengi, and its fruit (the flowers of this tree are highly fragrant; and the bark is used as a gargle for swollen gums).
more: n. f. The tree and its fruit; Mimusops elengī , Wat. The bark is used as a gargle for swollen gums.
s. f. Name of a tree. (Mimusops elengi).

ety: pass. part. of ولد 'to give birth to'
def: part. adj. Generated, born; — s.m. A son; — nativity, birthday; — celebration of the anniversary of the prophet Moḥammad's birth (also maulūd sharīf); — poetry chauted before the bier of a deceased person when carried out, burial-service.
more: n. m. Celebration of the anniversary of Mahommed's birth.
(part. pass. of ولد) 1. adj. Born, generated; a son. 2. s. m. Poetry chanted before the bier of a deceased person when carried out, burial-service.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic مَوْلُود (mawlūd).

def: s.m. One who chants the maulūd; a mourner.
more: A person who chants before the bier of a corpse.

def: s.f. A honey-bee.

s.m. = مو mau, 'honey,' q.v. s.f. Mother, &c. ( = mā, and māʼī, qq.v.). s.m. (in Prosody) A molossus or metrical foot of three long syllables; — a magical or mystical formula; a name of Brahmā, of Vishṇu, and of Śiva.
more: n. m. 1. Silence; taciturnity. 2. The office or position of a munī or holy sage. 4. (Rus. moman ) Butter mixed with flour before kneading it. maun birt , A vow of silence or its observance. maunā-rānī , n. f. The queen of silence; a silent woman ( an-bolā rānī ). maun sādhnā , v. n. 1. To remain silent; to practise taciturnity. 2. To make no reply.
s. m. Silence, taciturnity. مون گـﮩنا maun gahnā, or مون سادهنا maun sādhnā, To remain silent, to practise taciturnity.

def: (fr. ʻaun, q.v.; or (virtually) pl. of maʻūnai), s.m. = P معونت maʻūnat (for A. معونة, fr. ʻaun,q.v.) s.f. Aid, assistance; favour; protection.
more: (from عون) s. m. Aid, assistance, favour: or, plur. of معونت maʼūﻌnat, q. v.

def: s.m. Silence, taciturnity; — the office or position of a Muni or holy sage; — adj. Silent, speechless
subs: vulg. maun-birt, s.m. A vow (or the observance of a vow) of silence; — adj. Observing a vow of silence; holding (one's) tongue
or maunā-rānī, s.f. 'Queen of silence,' a silent woman
or maun gahnā, v.n. To practise silence or taciturnity, to remain silent; to refrain from replying (to), to make no reply.

ety: prob. fr. S. मृत्, 'earth, clay'; — cf. S. मोणः 'a basket'
def: s.m. A large jar; — a large (round) basket.
more: n. m. 1. ( Farrukh .) A large jar. 2. (E.) A large basket.
s. m. 1. A large jar. 2. A basket.

def: (for A. معونة, fr. ʻaun, q.v.) s.f. = A معون maʻūn (fr. ʻaun, q.v.; or (virtually)pl. of maʻūnai), s.m. Aid, assistance; favour; protection.
more: (infin. of عون Heb. ) s. f. Assistance, protection, favour.

def: s.f. = maun, q.v. (fr. ʻaun, q.v.; or (virtually) pl. of maʻūnai), s.m. = P معونت maʻūnat (for A. معونة, fr. ʻaun,q.v.) s.f. Aid, assistance; favour; protection.
